prompt: In BACnet, which network layer message is used to discover the address of a remote device when only its device instance number is known?

options.text: Who-Is
ReadProperty
SubscribeCOV
WriteProperty

explanation: Who-Is is the BACnet broadcast/unbroadcast discovery service; devices respond with I-Am containing their network address and device instance.

distractorExplanations.b: ReadProperty reads object data after the device address is already known.
distractorExplanations.c: SubscribeCOV registers for change-of-value notifications, not address discovery.
distractorExplanations.d: WriteProperty modifies object values and requires a known device address.

prompt: A BACnet MS/TP trunk shows frequent token-pass failures. Which physical-layer issue is the most common root cause?

options.text: Incorrect EIA-485 biasing or termination at segment ends
Duplicate IP addresses on the BBMD
Missing TLS certificates on the supervisory server
ASHRAE Guideline 36 economizer lockout setpoint

explanation: MS/TP uses EIA-485; missing termination or bias resistors causes reflections and framing errors that break token passing.

distractorExplanations.b: BBMD and IP conflicts affect BACnet/IP segments, not MS/TP token passing.
distractorExplanations.c: TLS applies to IT-layer security, not MS/TP physical token integrity.
distractorExplanations.d: Control sequences do not cause MS/TP physical-layer token failures.

prompt: Which BACnet object property carries the human-readable label shown on most operator graphics for an Analog Input?

options.text: Object_Name
Object_Identifier
Protocol_Version
Segmentation_Supported

explanation: Object_Name is the standard BACnet property for descriptive labels; Object_Identifier is the numeric type-instance key.

distractorExplanations.b: Object_Identifier is the unique numeric key (type, instance), not the display label.
distractorExplanations.c: Protocol_Version describes the BACnet revision supported by the device.
distractorExplanations.d: Segmentation_Supported indicates message segmentation capability, not naming.

prompt: A VAV box is hunting between minimum and maximum damper position at part load. Which tuning change is the best first step?

options.text: Reduce proportional gain or lengthen the integral time on the airflow loop
Disable the room temperature sensor
Increase supply fan static pressure setpoint by 2 in. w.c.
Lock the damper at 50% open permanently

explanation: Hunting at part load usually indicates overly aggressive airflow PI tuning; reducing gain or slowing integral action stabilizes damper modulation.

distractorExplanations.b: Removing zone temperature feedback eliminates comfort control and does not fix airflow loop tuning.
distractorExplanations.c: Raising static pressure can worsen hunting and increase fan energy without addressing loop gains.
distractorExplanations.d: Fixing the damper defeats variable-air-volume control and comfort response.

prompt: Under ASHRAE Guideline 36, when should economizer dampers typically remain at minimum position?

options.text: When outdoor air is unsuitable for free cooling or the system is not in economizer-capable mode
Whenever indoor CO₂ is below 800 ppm
Only during occupied heating mode regardless of outdoor conditions
Whenever the supply fan is off, even if the return fan runs

explanation: Guideline 36 sequences lock out or minimize economizer operation when outdoor conditions do not support free cooling or when interlocks require minimum ventilation only.

distractorExplanations.b: Low CO₂ alone does not force minimum position if free cooling is available and permitted.
distractorExplanations.c: Heating mode may still allow ventilation minimums, but outdoor suitability drives economizer position.
distractorExplanations.d: Fan interlocks vary by design; minimum position is driven by economizer suitability, not fan status alone.

prompt: A hot-water valve is wired for fail-open and the controller loses power during winter. What is the intended safe default?

options.text: The valve opens to provide heat and reduce freeze risk
The valve closes to save energy
The valve modulates to 50% based on last BACnet command
The valve position becomes undefined until power returns

explanation: Fail-open heating valves are selected so loss of actuator power allows flow to coils or radiation, reducing freeze damage risk in cold climates.

distractorExplanations.b: Fail-closed on heating coils increases freeze risk during power loss.
distractorExplanations.c: Spring-return or pneumatic actuators drive to a fixed fail position, not last command, on power loss.
distractorExplanations.d: Engineered fail positions are defined precisely for life-safety and freeze protection.

prompt: Which sensor placement best supports accurate discharge-air temperature control on a rooftop unit?

options.text: Downstream of the heating and cooling coils in a well-mixed airstream
Inside the outdoor air hood before the filters
On the compressor discharge line
In the return plenum only

explanation: Discharge-air sensors must read fully mixed supply air after conditioning coils to give the control loop representative temperature feedback.

distractorExplanations.b: Outdoor air hood location measures ambient intake, not discharge temperature.
distractorExplanations.c: Compressor discharge line temperature reflects refrigerant conditions, not supply-air delivery.
distractorExplanations.d: Return plenum temperature does not represent conditioned discharge air.

prompt: An operator needs to verify that night setback restored occupied setpoints at 6:00 a.m. Which BAS feature provides the fastest audit trail?

options.text: Trend log of zone temperature setpoint and mode with timestamp
Annual energy benchmark report only
Network switch port mirroring
Static PDF as-built drawing set

explanation: Time-stamped trend logs of setpoints and modes show exactly when schedules and overrides changed after setback.

distractorExplanations.b: Annual benchmarks summarize energy use but do not show daily schedule transitions.
distractorExplanations.c: Switch mirroring captures network packets, not interpreted HVAC setpoint history.
distractorExplanations.d: As-built drawings document design intent, not live schedule execution.

prompt: Which practice best reduces nuisance alarms on a BAS without hiding real faults?

options.text: Add reasonable off-delay and deadband to analog alarms after validating thresholds with operations staff
Disable all priority-3 alarms permanently
Route every alarm to email with no grouping
Increase alarm limits until zero alarms occur

explanation: Off-delay and deadband prevent chattering alarms while preserving meaningful limits agreed with operators.

distractorExplanations.b: Blanket disabling hides faults and violates good operations practice.
distractorExplanations.c: Ungrouped email floods increase alarm fatigue without fixing root causes.
distractorExplanations.d: Setting limits so wide that no alarm ever occurs defeats monitoring purpose.

prompt: A BAS server clock is 7 minutes ahead of domain time. What is the most likely operational impact?

options.text: Scheduled events and trend timestamps appear early relative to real time
BACnet device instance numbers will duplicate
MS/TP token passing stops immediately
VFD motor direction reverses

explanation: BAS schedules, logs, and correlation with utility meters depend on accurate time; clock drift makes events appear early or late.

distractorExplanations.b: Device instance assignment is independent of server clock sync.
distractorExplanations.c: MS/TP token passing is a field-bus function unaffected by server NTP drift alone.
distractorExplanations.d: Motor rotation is determined by VFD wiring and parameters, not BAS clock skew.

prompt: Before granting a temporary contractor login to the BAS, what is the minimum security step?

options.text: Issue a time-limited account with role-based permissions scoped to required equipment
Share the administrator password to avoid setup delays
Disable all alarms for the building for the week
Publish BACnet/IP port 47808 to the public internet

explanation: Least-privilege, time-bound contractor accounts limit exposure while allowing required commissioning or service work.

distractorExplanations.b: Shared admin credentials violate least privilege and audit requirements.
distractorExplanations.c: Disabling alarms removes operational visibility and is unrelated to access control.
distractorExplanations.d: Exposing BACnet directly to the internet creates unnecessary cyber risk.

prompt: An operator exports monthly meter data from the BAS. Which file format is most suitable for import into a spreadsheet for pivot analysis?

options.text: CSV with column headers and ISO timestamps
Proprietary binary backup of the controller firmware
JPEG screenshot of the trend chart
MS/TP token statistics dump

explanation: CSV with headers and consistent timestamps imports cleanly into Excel or Google Sheets for analysis and pivot tables.

distractorExplanations.b: Firmware backups are for restoration, not tabular energy analysis.
distractorExplanations.c: Images are not machine-readable for spreadsheet calculations.
distractorExplanations.d: Token statistics relate to field-bus diagnostics, not meter trending.

prompt: Which KPI best indicates persistent simultaneous heating and cooling at an air-handling unit?

options.text: Heating and cooling valves both commanded above minimum for a sustained period while discharge air sits near setpoint
Supply fan status equals ON once per day
Outdoor air temperature below 0°F for one hour
Filter differential pressure alarm latched for 5 seconds

explanation: Sustained overlap of heating and cooling valve commands near discharge setpoint is a classic fighting signal wasting energy.

distractorExplanations.b: Daily fan operation is normal and does not indicate fighting.
distractorExplanations.c: Cold outdoor air alone does not prove simultaneous heating and cooling.
distractorExplanations.d: Short filter alarms indicate maintenance needs, not valve fighting.

prompt: A campus BAS uses hierarchical navigation: site → building → system → equipment. What is the primary operator benefit?

options.text: Faster location of points and alarms without searching the entire network flat list
Elimination of all BACnet routing requirements
Automatic VAV box rebalancing
Guaranteed zero cyber intrusion risk

explanation: Hierarchical organization mirrors physical assets so operators drill down to relevant alarms, trends, and setpoints quickly.

distractorExplanations.b: Navigation hierarchy is a UI construct; BACnet routers are still required where networks segment.
distractorExplanations.c: Airflow balancing requires test-and-adjust procedures, not graphics hierarchy alone.
distractorExplanations.d: Navigation structure does not by itself provide cybersecurity guarantees.

prompt: During functional performance testing, a chiller enable interlock fails because the BAS points to the wrong binary input. What is the correct commissioning response?

options.text: Document the deficiency, correct the I/O mapping, and retest the interlock before acceptance
Force the chiller ON in software and skip the interlock test
Accept the system because the chiller starts manually
Disable the interlock permanently in code

explanation: Commissioning requires verified I/O mapping and successful retest of safety and enable interlocks before turnover.

distractorExplanations.b: Software forcing bypasses the failed test without fixing the root mapping error.
distractorExplanations.c: Manual operation does not prove automatic interlock function.
distractorExplanations.d: Removing interlocks creates safety and warranty issues.

prompt: Which document best records expected versus actual sequence behavior for a rooftop unit economizer during commissioning?

options.text: Sequence of operations test script with step, expected result, and pass/fail columns
Vendor marketing brochure
Employee phone directory
Unlabeled conduit routing sketch

explanation: Commissioning test scripts tie each sequence step to expected outcomes and document pass/fail evidence for turnover.

distractorExplanations.b: Marketing materials lack project-specific expected results.
distractorExplanations.c: Contact lists do not document control sequence verification.
distractorExplanations.d: Conduit sketches show routing, not economizer functional test results.
